raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2023, I signed up for a timeshare account with XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( XXXX ). At the same time, XXXX opened a Hilton American Express credit card account in my name to make a payment for the timeshare. ( Reference Credit card ending in XXXX in the name of XXXX XXXX XXXX for {$4000.00}. Am Ex reference number XXXX ) Soon thereafter and after consulting with an attorney, I was able to cancel the timeshare. The legal reasons for the termination were ( among others ), it was an unconscionable agreement, it lacked consideration, there was misrepresentation and a lack of attorney representation. The timeshare was never used. 

I transferred deeds back to XXXX and waived all my rights to the timeshare. 

Thereafter, effective XX/XX/2023, I received confirmation from XXXX that the timeshare contract had been cancelled and the associated vacation ownership interest had been transferred back to XXXX. Please see attached letter. 

The credit card was not used for any other purchase. We requested the credit card charge be taken off and the credit card could not be cancelled. On XX/XX/2023, we received a letter from American Express saying they could not remove the charge as the merchant had not cancelled the agreement. They urged us to resolve the matter directly with XXXX. 

XXXX has now cancelled the timeshare agreement. We have resolved the matter with the merchant per the direction of American Express. Therefore, there is no valid charge that should remain on the credit card. We have written American Express numerous times with no reply. 

Please have the charge removed from the credit card immediately so that my good credit can be restored. Thank you.
